notes: Reference EXRs rendered with the pinned pbrt v4 above. Tolerances reflect current measured parity (skinny's own default key light disabled; residual = RGB-vs-spectral + path-tracer-vs-sppm caustics) as a regression guard with headroom. Regenerate refs deliberately if the pbrt version or a scene changes.

scenes.name: bathroom
scenes.notes: Heavy scene from pbrt contemporary-bathroom. ref via: python tests/pbrt/regen_refs.py --scene bathroom --res 256 --spp 256 --integrator path (rendered with pbrt's PATH integrator to match skinny's path anchor; the authored sppm EXR is unclamped — max~2120, bypassing maxcomponentvalue — and pbrt's own path-vs-sppm differ by relMSE~0.63 on this caustic scene). FIREFLY CATASTROPHE FIXED (change film-maxcomponent-clamp): skinny now applies the pbrt film maxcomponentvalue=50 per-sample radiance clamp, so path|wavefront vs pbrt dropped 232.8 -> ~0.34 and bdpt-vs-path dropped 6612 -> 0.36 (MSE 0.017, structurally identical); skinny max 7985 -> 50; path megakernel==wavefront still 0.0000. STILL KNOWN-DIVERGENT for two residuals (each a separate follow-up that must LOWER these baselines): (1) pbrt-truth ~0.34 relMSE = RGB-vs-spectral / blackbody-emitter-normalization (skinny normalizes blackbody to unit-luminance, pbrt to peak-spectral-1); (2) self-consistency relMSE vs the path anchor stays large from dark-pixel /b^2 amplification on this noisy scene (BDPT MSE 0.017 but relMSE 0.36) plus a genuine sppm-vs-path estimator difference (sppm MSE 10.4, relMSE 65) — a metric-robustness follow-up. emissive-triangle-bsdf-mis (2026-06-24): path BSDF-hit MIS complement fixes area-light dim bias; bathroom mean 1.36->0.97, path FLIP 0.222->0.155 (megakernel==wavefront relMSE 0.0000). relMSE baseline 0.342 unchanged (exposure-aligned gate is brightness-blind; residual = RGB-vs-spectral). BDPT/SPPM/neural/restir baselines re-measure in their own changes (Path anchor moved brighter).

scenes.name: dragon
scenes.notes: Heavy subsurface dragon (28.8M tris → wavefront-only). ref via: python tests/pbrt/regen_refs.py --scene dragon --res 256 --spp 256. KNOWN-DIVERGENT (harness-first): subsurface brightness residual vs pbrt is a follow-up (see project_pbrt_subsurface_volumetric). Re-measure on a GPU host to pin the per-combo baseline.
